Notice Of Motion And Motion To Tax Costs And/Or To Strike Memoranda Of Csots Fro John J. Hartford And Albert Lee,
Off Calendar Mar-11-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Elizabeth Karnazes, Tel. 650.345.9200, plaintiff and moving party, appearing on her own behalf, via Court Call;
Albert Lee, Esq. of the Law Offices of Albert Lee, Tel. 415.215.6868, for defendant and opposing party.

RULING: A R G U E D. After argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF ELIZABETH KARNAZES' Motion To Tax Costs And/Or To Strike Memoranda Of Costs of John J. Hartford And Albert Lee is denied. The Memorandum of Costs ("Costs Memo") is timely. (CRC 3.1700(a)(1).) On January 25, 2012, the Court issued an order granting Defendant Hartford's motion to quash service of summons based on a lack of personal delivery. (Order issued, Jan. 25, 2012.) This is not a judgment or dismissal pursuant to Cal. Rules of Court, rule 3.1700(a)(1).) For Costs Memo Item no. 1, Defendant Hartford is entitled to filing costs. (CCP 1033.5(a)(1).) For Costs Memo Item no. 11, the Court determines that Defendant Hartford may recover costs for messenger services. (Foothill-De Anza Community College Dist. v. Emerich (2007) 158 Cal.App.4th 11, 30.) For Memorandum of Costs on Appeal ("Appeal Costs Memo") Item no. 1, Defendant Hartford is entitled to filing costs (CRC 8.278(d)(1)(A). For Appeal Costs Memo Item no. 4: Defendant Hartford is entitled to costs to print and reproduce his briefs. (CRC 8.278(d)(1)(E).) For Appeal Costs Memo Item no. 7, Defendant Hartford is entitled to service costs. (CRC 8.278(d)(1)(D).) For Appeal Costs Memo Item no. 8, Defendant Hartford is entitled to costs to serve his briefs and other papers. (CRC 8.278(d)(1)(D). Order signed. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR# 5976.

Off Calendar Dec-02-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: ELIZABETH KARNAZES, PLAINTIFF IN PRO PER.
ELIZABETH CELNIKER, ESQ., 925.288.1776, FOR DEFENDANTS SAINT PAUL SURPLUS LINES INSURANCE COMPANY, HARRY GILBERT AND TOM COLLINS.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: Plaintiff Elizabeth Karnazes' Motion To Tax Costs And/Or To Strike Memorandum Of Costs is Denied. Notice of Entry of Judgment was served on Plaintiff on August 27. The Memorandum of Costs was served on Plaintiff on August 30 and was therefore timely under Cal. Rule of Court 3.1700. By filing a proof of service, Defendants met their burden of showing that service was completed. (CCP 1013(a), "service is complete at the time of deposit in the mail.") The addressee incurs "the risk of the failure of the mail." (Silver v. McNamee (1966) 69 Cal.App.4th 269, 280.) Order signed in open court. Judge: A. DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-12-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Elizabeth Karnazes, Esq., 650.345.9200, plaintiff in pro per.
Elizabeth B. Celniker, Esq., 925.288.1776, for defendants Saint Paul Surplus Lines Insurance Company, Tom Collins and Harry Gilbert.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling with modification: DEFENDANTS TOM COLLINS, HARRY GILBERT, ST. PAUL SURPLUS LINES INSURANCE COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained with leave to amend as the fraud causes of action (causes of action 3 through 7). After having been given leave to amend to plead these causes of action with more specificity, including the allegation of facts showing justifiable reliance, Plaintiff has not pled these causes of action with the requisite specificity, and has failed to allege justifiable reliance. Further, the burden is on Plaintiff to show how the pleading can be amended, and she has failed to do so in her opposing papers. Counsel for defendant to submit proposed order. Judge: Marla Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Denise Doucette, #5963.
